editorial_angle: The Claude Code leak wasn't a breach — it was a serve. Bun handed out 512,000 lines of source code because that's what it does by default, and the bug had been sitting open for 20 days while millions of developers ran the same configuration. Same 48-hour window: Axios via stolen credentials npm trusted without question, LiteLLM via a poisoned PyPI publish that CI/CD pipelines auto-consumed. Three different exploits, zero sophistication required. The deeper story isn't KAIROS or dream mode — it's that the entire AI development supply chain runs on defaults nobody audits. The most advanced AI coding tool on earth leaked not because someone was clever, but because no one checked a checkbox.
key_message: The biggest source leak of 2026 didn't require an exploit — just the default settings everyone's been running.

supporting.name: paddo.dev — npm Had a Very Bad Day

visual_metaphor_direction.core_tension: A vault built to protect the most sophisticated AI secrets on earth — left open not by a heist, but by a default setting nobody turned off. The security is real and elaborate; the failure is embarrassingly mundane.
key_elements: Anthropic or Claude branding on the vault/building
A wide-open door or window with source code visibly streaming out
A small, overlooked sign or switch labeled 'DEFAULT: ON' or 'Source Maps: Enabled'
Contrast between elaborate security (cameras, locks, reinforced walls) and the mundane open door
Optional: npm logo or Bun logo as a secondary element showing the systemic nature
avoid: Hackers in hoodies — this wasn't a hack
Dramatic red warning screens or explosion imagery — the irony is in the mundanity
Literal depictions of code on screens — too generic
Spy or espionage framing — wrong emotional register
Any imagery that implies user error by a developer — the failure is systemic/default, not human mistake

x.hook: Anthropic's source wasn't hacked. It was served.
x.angle: The bug was open 20 days. Nobody checked their defaults. This is the provocation that gets quote-tweets from developers who immediately went and checked their own .npmignore.

linkedin.hook: Three npm supply chain incidents in 48 hours. Zero required sophisticated exploits.
linkedin.angle: Framed for engineering managers and CTOs: the audit question. When did you last check what your build tool ships by default? Drives comments from people sharing their own close calls or audit processes.

bluesky.hook: I build with Bun. I checked my .npmignore within an hour of reading the HN thread.
bluesky.angle: Personal, peer-to-peer. Opens a genuine conversation about what other builders found when they looked. Webdevdad energy — not broadcasting, just talking to the community.

do_not_include: Claude Code v2.1.89 release notes (incremental update)
KAIROS or dream mode as the primary story — they're interesting but a distraction from the defaults angle
Any framing that makes this about Anthropic being careless specifically — the point is systemic npm defaults, not one company's mistake
